title: Koeleria tenella
taxonomy: Koeleria tenella is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a member of the tribe Poeae and the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Koeleria is a group of grasses that are commonly known as hair-grasses. They are native to the Northern Hemisphere and are commonly found in temperate regions. The classification of Koeleria tenella reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses within the family Poaceae.

intro: Koeleria tenella, also known as small hair-grass or small June-grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria tenella include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koeleria tenella is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory complaints.
